findings.evidence: grep hits: lines 145, 149, 153 define the encoding helpers; lines 161, 175, 193, 207, 229, 236, 249, 267, 291, 336 expose proof lemmas used only by `ideal_ncard_le_prodLeTuples_ncard`/`card_ideal_absNorm_le`.
findings.fix: Make the encoding machinery private or move it to an internal namespace with only the needed final theorem exported.
findings.issue: Implementation details of the injection are public (`ratBelow`, `primeCoord`, `encodeIdeal`, and their recovery/injectivity lemmas), although the roadmap target only needs the final ideal-count bound.

findings.evidence: grep shows `def prodLeTuples` at line 40 and only `prodLeTuples_finite`/`prodLeTuples_ncard_le`; no `mem_prodLeTuples` or `*_def` restatement is provided.
findings.fix: Either make `prodLeTuples` and its count lemmas private/internal, or add a characteristic `@[simp] theorem mem_prodLeTuples` and keep the public tuple API intentionally documented.
findings.issue: `prodLeTuples` is exported as a new public set definition but has no public membership/simp API, so downstream users must unfold its body to use it.

summary: The main bound is present, but the PR exports the whole encoding/counting implementation as public API without a downstream need or characteristic API.
